Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Vectral Systems renewal threads, Rachel Whitfield, and the counter-offer to understand the current negotiation status. Discover that Rachel demanded 15% discount on April 18 citing three service failures, MoveOps counter-offered 10% + Preferred Partner Program, board meeting was ~April 23, and no response has been received since.
OE 2: Search CRM for Vectral Systems deals and engagement notes to find the renewal deal status. Discover the deal "Vectral Systems, Q3 2026 Contract Renewal (At Risk)" at dealstage at_risk, with full negotiation timeline and note that Vectral is actively evaluating two competitor relocation providers.
OE 3: Search Slack for internal Vectral renewal discussions to find any team conversations about strategy or follow-up plans.
OE 4: Search Airtable relocation records for Vectral Systems to check how recent moves went. Find Kevin Tran (Portland-Denver, completed), Danielle Osei (Phoenix-Denver, completed), Daniel Reeves (Seattle-San Diego, in progress), Maya Johansson (Portland-San Diego, in progress), and one cancelled relocation.
OE 5: Search Airtable stipend records for Vectral Systems to check the "stipend mess." Discover that 3 of 5 false rejections from the ExpenseBot pilot are STILL unresolved: Maria Santos ($550 standing desk, still rejected), James Okafor ($82 keyboard tray, still rejected), and Lisa Park ($130 WiFi system, still rejected). Danielle Osei ($475 chair) and Kevin Tran ($185 internet) were manually corrected by Mina.
OE 6: Search Airtable stipend records for Mosaic Robotics (the other pilot client) to get the full expense tool picture. Discover false approvals: Andre Washington's standing desk ($1,200, $400 over $800 cap) and coworking ($350, excluded category), Kenji Nakamura's coworking ($135, same exclusion bug). MoveOps absorbing $885 per Elena's directive.
OE 7: Search emails for the ExpenseBot audit results and Samira's remediation updates. Discover the audit found 47 expenses total: 83% correct, 5 false rejections (all Vectral), 3 false approvals (all Mosaic). Auto-decisions disabled since March 18.
OE 8: Search emails and Slack for duplicate receipt findings from Dmitri's data quality audit. Discover that Kevin Tran (Vectral) and Andre Washington (Mosaic) submitted byte-for-byte identical $89 keyboard receipt PDFs (same SHA-256 hash). Kevin's was paid out on March 8. Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop) submitted a third receipt from the same PDF template. AM verification requests were sent to Rachel Whitfield at Vectral , adding more friction to the strained relationship.
OE 9: Search Linear for ExpenseBot-related engineering tickets and the duplicate detection issue. Find issue #220 (stipend duplicate detection, assigned to Dmitri, status To Do) and the remediation tickets (ENG-216 audit, ENG-217 pipeline fix, ENG-218 dashboard validation).
OE 10: Search emails for the DOT compliance inquiry and PHMSA case. Discover the formal inquiry (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187), 15-day extension granted, deadline June 5, 2026, potential penalties up to $59,017/day.
OE 11: Search Airtable relocation records for entries involving hazmat materials (filtering by special requirements containing "hazmat," "DOT," "Class 9," or "lithium"). Find three active hazmat relocations: (1) Mei-Lin Zhao for GreenStack Energy, lithium-ion battery test modules, Class 9, DOT-certified transport required; (2) Keiko Tanaka for Mosaic Robotics industrial laser cutter with Class 3B CO2 laser tube, DOT placarding required; (3) Raj Patel for NorthWind Technologies, rack-mounted servers with lithium UPS batteries, Class 9, chain-of-custody required.
OE 12: Search CRM deals for the three hazmat relocations to check documentation status and any noted gaps.
OE 13: Search Linear and Slack for any existing DOT compliance or hazmat documentation tracking tickets.
OE 14: Look up David Chen's and Marcus Thorne's contact details using contacts search to get their email addresses (david.chen@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com).
OE 15: Send the findings to David Chen and Marcus Thorne (via send_email or conversations_add_message) summarizing the Vectral account status, expense tool situation, DOT compliance readiness, and any other discovered issues.
OE 16: Create Linear tickets (via linear_create_issue) for any discovered issues that are not currently being tracked hazmat documentation gaps, unresolved Vectral stipend corrections, or other untracked problems found during investigation.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
